summary: Make actor-of--limit-queue-size deterministic by blocking the worker before the bounded-queue stress loop, instead of relying on (sleep 0.05) timing.

items.title: Deflake actor-of--limit-queue-size
items.description:
Rewrite the test `actor-of--limit-queue-size` in tests/actor-test.lisp so it no longer depends on the dispatcher being slower than the 10-tell loop. Root cause: with `:queue-size 1`, the test relies on no message being popped from the actor's bounded queue during the 10 tells. On slower Lisps (e.g. clamiga), the shared-dispatcher worker pops the first item before the loop finishes, frees the queue slot, and a second tell succeeds — yielding the observed 2 successes / 8 failures instead of 1 / 9. Sleeping longer inside receive does NOT fix this, because the race is between push #1 and pop #1, not between pop and receive completion.

The fix is structural: hold the message-box lock during the 10-tell loop by entering the user receive function on a `:block` message and parking there. Because `dispatcher-exec-fun` (src/mbox/message-box.lisp) wraps both popq and handle-popped-item in `(bt2:acquire-lock lock :wait t) ... (bt2:release-lock lock)`, no other worker can pop from the actor's queue while a receive is in progress. Pushes from the test thread continue to work because `pushq` uses the bounded queue's own lock, not the msgbox lock.

Concretely, replace the existing test body with this shape (keeping the test name, docstring, suite, and final `(ac:shutdown sys)` cleanup):

  (test actor-of--limit-queue-size
    "Tests that actor's queue size can be limited."
    (let ((sys (asys:make-actor-system))
          (started-p nil)
          (release-p nil))
      (unwind-protect
           (let ((actor (actor-of sys
                                  :receive (lambda (msg)
                                             (declare (ignore msg))
                                             (setf started-p t)
                                             (await-cond 10.0 release-p))
                                  :queue-size 1)))
             ;; Park the actor's worker inside receive so it holds the
             ;; msgbox lock for the duration of the loop below. While the
             ;; lock is held no message can be popped from the bounded
             ;; queue, so exactly one tell can fit and the other nine
             ;; deterministically fail with queue-full-error.
             (tell actor :block)
             (is-true (await-cond 1.0 started-p))
             (let ((tells (loop :repeat 10
                                :collect (ignore-errors
                                          (tell actor "run")))))
               (format t "tells: ~a~%" tells)
               (is (= 1 (length (filter (lambda (x) (if x x)) tells))))
               (is (= 9 (length (filter #'null tells)))))
             (setf release-p t))
        (ac:shutdown sys))))

Notes for the implementer:
- `await-cond` is already imported in the defpackage at the top of the file (line 6).
- `assert-cond`/`await-cond` poll every 50ms; the worker parking in await-cond is acceptable here because the goal is exactly to hold the lock.
- Do NOT change the dispatcher (leave `:shared` as default); the lock semantics in `dispatcher-exec-fun` are what makes the fix work, and they apply to all dp dispatchers.
- Do NOT introduce a bt2 dependency in the test — the polling/flag pattern using `await-cond` is sufficient and matches the style used elsewhere in this file (e.g. lines 101, 150, 217).
- After releasing, the worker will return from receive, then pop and process the single queued "run" tell (started-p is already t, release-p is already t, so the second receive call returns almost immediately), and ac:shutdown will tear the system down cleanly.
- Keep the diagnostic `(format t "tells: ~a~%" tells)` line so failures remain easy to interpret.
- Note: the request title says "two tests" but the failure report shows two `(is ...)` checks inside the single test `actor-of--limit-queue-size`. There is only one test to fix.
